why: The move Qxg6+ is a forcing check that wins material. After ...Rxg6, White recaptures with Rxg6, leaving Black's king exposed and down a rook. items.answer_explanation: This is a forced winning move. After Qxh7+, Black's king has only one legal move: Kxh7. Then White plays Rh3+, forcing the king to g8 (Kh7–g8), and then Rh8# delivers checkmate. The combination exploits the pin on the h-file and the exposed position of Black's king.

items.answer_explanation: The move Qxg6+ is a forcing check that wins material immediately. After ...hxg6, White captures the rook on h8 with Rh4# (or Rh1# after ...Kg7), delivering checkmate. This move exploits the alignment of the black king and queen on the g-file and the rook lift potential on the h-file.
